scenarios.description: Calling setScores with null should throw NullPointerException for scores parameter
scenarios.precondition: MiniMaxAlgorithm instance created via new MiniMaxAlgorithm(); no stubs or mocks
scenarios.expectedResult: throws NullPointerException; no change to existing scores or height
pseudocode: GIVEN a MiniMaxAlgorithm instance and int[] scores = null

scenarios.description: Calling setScores with non-power-of-two length triggers rejection branch ($stack3 != 0)
scenarios.precondition: MiniMaxAlgorithm instance with initial scores captured; no mocks
scenarios.expectedResult: scores and height remain unchanged; printed message "The number of scores must be a power of 2." to System.out
pseudocode: GIVEN a MiniMaxAlgorithm instance and int[] scores = new int[3]
THEN verify instance.getScores() is unchanged and System.out contains "The number of scores must be a power of 2."

scenarios.description: Calling setScores with length=1 (2^0) follows valid branch and sets scores and height=0
scenarios.precondition: MiniMaxAlgorithm instance with initial scores captured; no mocks
pseudocode: GIVEN a MiniMaxAlgorithm instance and int[] scores = new int[1]
THEN assert instance.getScores() == scores and instance.getHeight() == 0

scenarios.description: Calling setScores with length=8 (2^3) follows valid branch and sets scores and height=3
scenarios.precondition: MiniMaxAlgorithm instance with initial scores captured; no mocks
pseudocode: GIVEN a MiniMaxAlgorithm instance and int[] scores = new int[8]
THEN assert instance.getScores() == scores and instance.getHeight() == 3
